volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 3.5.767 - Section 4.2.857)

sections.label: Section 3.5.767
sections.original: Mementote illud , advocati .
sections.translation: Remember that , assistants .

sections.label: Section 3.5.767b
sections.original: Meminimus .
sections.translation: We will remember it .

sections.label: Section 3.5.768
sections.original: Hahahae , iam teneo quid sit , perspexi modo .
sections.translation: Ha , ha , ha ! I now understand how it is , I ’ ve this instant seen through it .

sections.label: Section 3.5.769
sections.original: hi qui illum dudum conciliaverunt mihi
sections.translation: These persons , who a short time since introduced that Spartan stranger to me , their brain is now fired at it ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.771
sections.original: me esse hos trecentos Philippos facturum lucri .
sections.translation: because I ’ m going to make a gain of these three hundred Philippean pieces ; now , because they knew that this person was an enemy of mine , they have set him on to say that his slave , together with his gold , is in my house . It ’ s a planned contrivance

sections.label: Section 3.5.775
sections.original: ut eo me privent atque inter se dividant .
sections.translation: for them to deprive me of it , and to divide it among themselves . They are wanting to get away the lamb from the wolf [*] . They are wasting their pains .

sections.label: Section 3.5.777
sections.original: Negasne apud te esse aurum , nec servom meum ?
sections.translation: What , do you deny that either the gold or my slave is at your house ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.778
sections.original: Nego : et negando , si quid refert , ravio .
sections.translation: I do deny it ; and , if it ’ s of any use , I make myself hoarse with denying it .

sections.label: Section 3.5.779
sections.original: Periisti , leno . nam istest huius vilicus
sections.translation: You are undone , Procurer ; for that person

sections.label: Section 3.5.780
sections.original: quem tibi nos esse Spartiatam diximus ,
sections.translation: whom we told you was a Spartan , is his bailiff ; who brought you just now the three hundred Philippean pieces ; and that same gold , too , is in his purse .

sections.label: Section 3.5.783
sections.original: Vae vostrae aetati .
sections.translation: Woe unto you !

sections.label: Section 3.5.783b
sections.original: Id quidem in mundo est tuae .
sections.translation: That , indeed , is close at hand for yourself .

sections.label: Section 3.5.784
sections.original: Age omitte actutum , furcifer , marsuppium :
sections.translation: Come , you hang-dog , give up the purse this instant .

sections.label: Section 3.5.785
sections.original: manifesto fur es mihi . quaeso hercle , operam date ,
sections.translation: You are clearly a thief ; caught by me in the fact . ( To the ADVOCATI .) By my troth , I do beg of you , lend me your aid , so as to see me bring my slave out of his house .

sections.label: Section 3.5.787
sections.original: Nunc pol ego perii certo , haud arbitrario .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m now undone for certain , beyond a doubt ! This has been done on purpose that a snare might be laid for me . But why do I hesitate to betake me hence to utter perdition ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.790
sections.original: prius quam hínc optorto collo ad praetorem trahor ?
sections.translation: before I ’ m dragged off to the Praetor by the throat ? Alas ! what soothsayers I ’ ve been having for my diviners , who , if they promise anything that ’ s fair , it comes to pass but slowly ; that which they promise as unfortunate , comes directly . Now I ’ ll be off : I ’ ll consult my friends in what way —

sections.label: Section 3.5.795
sections.original: suspendere aequom censeant potissimum .—
sections.translation: they deem it best in especial for me to hang myself .

sections.label: Section 3.6.796
sections.original: Age tu progredere , ut testes videant te ire istinc foras .
sections.translation: Be off you , get out you , that the witnesses may see you coining out from here . Isn ’ t this my servant ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.797b
sections.original: Sum hercle vero , Agorastocles .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I really am , Agorastocles .

sections.label: Section 3.6.798
sections.original: Quid nunc , sceleste leno ?
sections.translation: How now , villanous Procurer ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.798b
sections.original: Quicum litigas
sections.translation: He , with whom you have the dispute , has made off .

sections.label: Section 3.6.799b
sections.original: Vtinam hinc abierit malam crucem .
sections.translation: I hope he ’ s gone hence to utter perdition .

sections.label: Section 3.6.800
sections.original: Ita nos velle aequom est .
sections.translation: It ’ s proper that we should wish the same .

sections.label: Section 3.6.800b
sections.original: Cras subscribam homini dicam .
sections.translation: To-morrow I ’ ll bring my action against the fellow .

sections.label: Section 3.6.801
sections.original: Numquid me ?
sections.translation: Anything further with me —?

sections.label: Section 3.6.801b
sections.original: Apscedas , sumas ornatum tuom .
sections.translation: You may go ; put on your own dress .

sections.label: Section 3.6.802
sections.original: Non sum nequiquam miles factus ; paululum
sections.translation: It wasn ’ t for nothing that I turned soldier . I made a little booty in-doors . While the household of the Procurer was asleep , I got myself well filled with the entrails .

sections.label: Section 3.6.805
sections.original: abscedam hinc intro .—
sections.translation: I ’ ll be off from here in-doors .

sections.label: Section 3.6.805b
sections.original: Factum a vobis comiter .
sections.translation: ’ Twas kindly done by you . Assistants , you have lent me your good services . To-morrow morning I beg you ’ ll meet me at the court of justice . ( To COLLYBISCUS .) Do you follow me in-doors . ( To the ASSISTANTS .) To you , farewell !

sections.label: Section 3.6.808b
sections.original: Et tu vale .
sections.translation: And you , farewell ! ( AGORASTOCLES goes into his house , followed by COLLYBISCUS .) This fellow wants a thing that ’ s notoriously unfair ;

sections.label: Section 3.6.810
sections.original: nostro servire nos sibi censet cibo .
sections.translation: he thinks that we are to serve him at our own expense . But such are all these rich people of ours : if you do anything of service , their thanks are lighter than a feather ; if there ’ s any offence , they show vengeance like lead . Let ’ s now go to our houses , if you like , forthwith ,

sections.label: Section 3.6.815
sections.original: quando id , quoi rei operam dedimus , impetravimus ,
sections.translation: since we ’ ve effected that for which we lent our services , to ruin this corrupter of our fellow-citizens .

sections.label: Section 4.1.817
sections.original: Exspécto quo pactó meae techinaé processuraé sient .
sections.translation: I ’ m awaiting in what way my plot is to proceed . I ’ m bent upon ruining this Procurer , since he torments my afflicted master ; but he in his turn beats me , and strikes me with his fist and heels .

sections.label: Section 4.1.820
sections.original: servire amanti miseria est , praesertim qui quod amat caret .
sections.translation: It ’ s a misery to be in the service of one who is in love , especially one who is debarred from the object which he woos . Heyday ! I see Syncerastus , the Procurer ’ s servant , betaking himself from the Temple . I ’ ll listen to what he has to say .

sections.label: Section 4.2.823
sections.original: Sátis spectatum est , deos atque homines eius neglegere gratiam ,
sections.translation: It ’ s quite clear that Gods and men neglect the benefit of him who has a master like a person of such character as I have for a master .

sections.label: Section 4.2.825
sections.original: neque periurior neque peior alter usquam est gentium ,
sections.translation: There ’ s not another person anywhere in the world more perjured or more wicked than is my master , nor one so filthy or so defiled with dirt . So may the Gods bless me , I ’ d rather pass my life either in the stone quarries or at the mill , with my sides hampered with heavy irons , than pass this servitude with a Procurer . What a race this is !

sections.label: Section 4.2.830
sections.original: quae illic hominum corruptelae fiunt . di vostram fidem ,
sections.translation: What corruptors of men they are ! Ye Gods , by our hopes in you , every kind of men you may see there , just as though you had come to Acheron — horse and foot , a freed-man , a thief , or a runaway , if you choose , one whipped , chained , or condemned to slavery . He who has got money to pay , whatever sort of person he is — all kinds are taken in ; throughout all the house , in consequence ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.835
sections.original: tenebrae latebrae , bibitur estur quasi in popina , hau secus .
sections.translation: are darkened spots and hiding-places : drinking and eating are going on , just as though in a cookshop , and in no less degree . There may you see epistles written in letters inscribed on pottery [*] , and sealed with pitch : the names are upon them in letters a cubit long ; such a perfect levy on vintners [*] have we got at our house .

sections.label: Section 4.2.839
sections.original: Omnia edepol mira sunt , nisi erus hunc heredem facit ,
sections.translation: Upon my faith , it is quite wonderful , if his master doesn ’ t make him his heir ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.840
sections.original: nam id quidem , illi , uti meditatur , verba faciet mortuo .
sections.translation: for really , the way he soliloquizes , he ’ s making a speech over him as though dead and gone . I ’ d both like to accost the fellow , and yet I listen to him with extreme delight .

sections.label: Section 4.2.842
sections.original: Haec quom hic video fieri , crucior : pretiis emptos maxumis ,
sections.translation: When I see these things going on , I ’ m vexed that slaves , purchased at the heaviest price , should at our house be robbed of the savings [*] which ought to go to their masters . But at last nothing is left visible : badly gotten , badly gone .

sections.label: Section 4.2.845
sections.original: Proinde habet orationem , quasi ipse sit frugi bonae ,
sections.translation: This man goes on talking quite as though he himself were an honest fellow , when , upon my faith , he himself is able to make worthlessness more worthless .

sections.label: Section 4.2.847
sections.original: Nunc domum haec ab aede Veneris refero vasa , ubi hostiis
sections.translation: Now I ’ m taking home these vessels from the Temple of Venus , where with his sacrifice my master has not been able to propitiate Venus on her festive day .

sections.label: Section 4.2.849
sections.original: Lepidam Venerem .
sections.translation: Charming Venus !

sections.label: Section 4.2.849b
sections.original: Nam meretrices nostrae primis hostiis
sections.translation: But our Courtesans , with their first victims , appeased Venus in an instant .

sections.label: Section 4.2.850b
sections.original: O lepidam Venerem denuo .
sections.translation: O charming Venus , once again !

sections.label: Section 4.2.851
sections.original: Nunc domum ibo .
sections.translation: Now I ’ ll go home .

sections.label: Section 4.2.851b
sections.original: Heus , Synceraste .
sections.translation: Hallo ! Syncerastus !

sections.label: Section 4.2.851c
sections.original: Syncerastum qui vocat ?
sections.translation: Who ’ s calling Syncerastus ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.852
sections.original: Tuos amicus .
sections.translation: Your friend .

sections.label: Section 4.2.852b
sections.original: Haud amice facis , qui cum onere offers moram .
sections.translation: You don ’ t act like a friend , in causing me delay when I ’ ve got a burden .

sections.label: Section 4.2.853
sections.original: At ob hanc moram tibi reddam operam úbi voles , ubi iusseris .
sections.translation: But in return for this matter I ’ ll lend you my aid , when you please , and when you give me your commands . Consider the agreement signed .

sections.label: Section 4.2.854b
sections.original: Si futurumst , do tibi operam hanc .
sections.translation: If so it is to be , I ’ ll give you my services in this —

sections.label: Section 4.2.854c
sections.original: Quo modo ?
sections.translation: In what way ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.855
sections.original: Vt enim ubi mihi vapulandum sit , tu corium sufferas .
sections.translation: Why that , when I ’ m to have a beating , you yourself may substitute your hide .

sections.label: Section 4.2.856
sections.original: apage , nesció quid viri sis .
sections.translation: Get along with you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.856b
sections.original: Malus sum .
sections.translation: I ’ m good for nothing .

sections.label: Section 4.2.856c
sections.original: Tibi sis .
sections.translation: Be so to yourself , then .

sections.label: Section 4.2.856d
sections.original: Te volo .
sections.translation: I want you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.857
sections.original: At onus urget .
sections.translation: But my burden is pressing me .
